$comment: Branch-protection ruleset for `main`. Source-of-truth for AC-11 of slice 050. Apply via `gh api -X PUT repos/mgoodric/security-atlas/branches/main/protection --input .github/branch-protection.json` OR the equivalent GitHub MCP call. Keep this file in sync with reality — if the maintainer adjusts via the UI, regenerate via `gh api repos/mgoodric/security-atlas/branches/main/protection > .github/branch-protection.json`.
$rationale_required_signatures_off: Signed-commit enforcement adds friction for community drive-by contributors (commits via the GitHub UI editor are signed by the GitHub bot, but commits from clones need a configured signing key). DCO sign-off + Conventional-Commit lint give us provenance and changelog discipline without raising the contributor bar. Revisit if the contributor base grows past ~25 active committers and trust-signal demand changes.

restrictions.$comment: Restrict push to the maintainer (so direct push is blocked for anyone else) PLUS the release-please bot's GitHub App identity, so release PRs can be merged without a human bypass of the review path. Adjust the app slug if a different release-please install is used.

$verification.1: A test PR with a failing CI check must NOT be mergeable. Mergeability resolves only after all checks in required_status_checks.contexts return green.
$verification.2: A direct `git push origin main` from a clone (bypassing PR) MUST be rejected by the server.
$verification.3: A `git push --force origin main` MUST be rejected even for the maintainer (enforce_admins=true).
$verification.4: Approving a PR and then pushing a new commit MUST invalidate the prior approval (dismiss_stale_reviews=true).
